name: Numeric Pain Rating Scale
description: 11-point (0–10) self-reported numeric scale on which the patient rates the intensity of their pain

en.name: Numeric Pain Rating Scale
en.shortDescription: 11-point (0–10) self-reported numeric scale on which the patient rates the intensity of their pain
en.longDescription: ## Definition & Clinical Context The Numeric Pain Rating Scale (NPRS, also Numeric Rating Scale or NRS) is an 11-point (0–10) self-reported scale on which the patient rates the intensity of their pain, where 0 corresponds to "no pain" and 10 to the "worst pain imaginable" [1]. The scale is widely used in clinical practice because of its simplicity, brevity, and applicability to a wide range of populations including patients unable to use a continuous visual analogue scale. The original validation study compared the NRS with a simple descriptive scale and the visual analogue scale, and demonstrated that the 11-point NRS performs better than both alternatives [1]. ## Included Concepts This concept set contains the OMOP-standard SNOMED concept for the scale: - `37151627` — Numeric Pain Rating Scale (`1284852002`, Staging / Scales, Standard) The non-standard SNOMED Observable Entity `37151630` (Numeric Pain Rating Scale score, `1284857008`) maps to this standard concept (OMOP `Maps to`), so recorded score values stored against the source code are captured through standard-concept resolution. ## Excluded Concepts The following SNOMED concept is intentionally **not** included: - `37174733` — Assessment using Numeric Pain Rating Scale (`1284856004`, Procedure) — represents the act of assessment, not the recorded score value ## References [1] Downie WW, Leatham PA, Rhind VM, Wright V, Branco JA, Anderson JA. Studies with pain rating scales. *Ann Rheum Dis*. 1978;37(4):378-381. doi:10.1136/ard.37.4.378. PMID: 686873

fr.name: Numeric Pain Rating Scale
fr.shortDescription: Échelle numérique auto-rapportée à 11 points (0–10) sur laquelle le patient cote l'intensité de sa douleur
fr.longDescription: ## Définition et contexte clinique La NPRS (Numeric Pain Rating Scale, ou échelle numérique NRS) est une échelle d'auto-évaluation à 11 points (0–10) sur laquelle le patient cote l'intensité de sa douleur, où 0 correspond à « aucune douleur » et 10 à la « pire douleur imaginable » [1]. Elle est largement utilisée en pratique clinique du fait de sa simplicité, de sa brièveté et de son applicabilité à un large éventail de populations, y compris les patients ne pouvant pas utiliser une échelle visuelle analogique continue. L'étude princeps de validation a comparé la NRS à une échelle descriptive simple et à l'échelle visuelle analogique, et a montré que la NRS à 11 points est plus performante que ces deux alternatives [1]. ## Concepts inclus Ce concept set contient le concept SNOMED standard OMOP de l'échelle : - `37151627` — Numeric Pain Rating Scale (`1284852002`, Staging / Scales, Standard) Le concept SNOMED Observable Entity non-standard `37151630` (Numeric Pain Rating Scale score, `1284857008`) se mappe vers ce concept standard (`Maps to` OMOP) ; les valeurs de score enregistrées sous le code source sont donc capturées via la résolution vers le concept standard. ## Concepts exclus Le concept SNOMED suivant est intentionnellement **non inclus** : - `37174733` — Assessment using Numeric Pain Rating Scale (`1284856004`, Procedure) — représente l'acte d'évaluation, non la valeur du score enregistré ## Références [1] Downie WW, Leatham PA, Rhind VM, Wright V, Branco JA, Anderson JA. Studies with pain rating scales. *Ann Rheum Dis*. 1978;37(4):378-381. doi:10.1136/ard.37.4.378. PMID: 686873

createdByDetails.profession: Medical Informatics and Intensive Care Physician

versions.author: Boris Delange
versions.message: Removed SNOMED Staging/Scales concept 37151627 (Numeric Pain Rating Scale, Standard) and SNOMED Procedure concept 37174733 (Assessment using Numeric Pain Rating Scale). Rationale: this concept set is intended to identify recorded NPRS pain score values; only the Observable Entity (37151630, Numeric Pain Rating Scale score) is relevant. The Staging/Scales concept represents the tool and the Procedure concept represents the act of assessment, neither carries the score value.

versions.author: Boris Delange
versions.message: Switched to the standard SNOMED concept for the scale. A standard concept (the scale itself) is preferred over a more specific but non-standard 'score' concept, which should not be used directly in OMOP. Reverses the v1.1.1 removal of the Staging/Scales concept.
